data.title: Real Messenger Files Interim Results Showing Steeper Losses and Shrinking Cash
data.description:
End of Quarter Sale - 50% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

An update from Real Messenger ( (RMSG) ) is now available.

On March 31, 2026, Real Messenger Corporation filed a Form 6-K with the U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission, furnishing unaudited interim consolidated financial statements for the six months ended September 30, 2025 and 2024. The filing also included an operating and financial review covering the same period, providing investors with updated visibility into the company’s revenue, expenses, losses and capital position.

The statements show that as of September 30, 2025, Real Messenger’s total assets had declined to about $1.34 million from $4.05 million at March 31, 2025, while cash and cash equivalents fell sharply to $0.85 million. For the six months ended September 30, 2025, the company posted modest revenue of $25,602 and a net loss of roughly $2.38 million, underscoring continued operating losses and a shrinking cash buffer that are likely to be closely watched by shareholders and creditors.

More about Real Messenger

Real Messenger Corporation is a Cayman Islands–incorporated foreign private issuer with principal executive offices in Costa Mesa, California. The company is listed in the United States and reports under Form 20-F, positioning it within the U.S. public markets despite its offshore incorporation structure.

The latest update is out from Maris-Tech Ltd. ( (MTEK) ).

On March 31, 2026, Maris-Tech Ltd. announced that it has begun commercial activity in the Ukrainian drone video payload market, marking an expansion of its AI-powered edge video solutions into one of the world’s most demanding UAV theaters. The initial deployment, executed via a third-party Israeli intermediary that supplied systems to end users in Ukraine, leverages the company’s field-proven Jupiter product line to deliver real-time video processing and onboard AI for intelligence gathering and situational awareness missions.

By targeting Ukraine’s drone ecosystem, where rapid adoption of advanced video and AI technologies is being driven by intensive operational needs, Maris-Tech is positioning its Jupiter platform as a core enabler of real-time intelligence and decision-making at the edge. The move underscores the company’s strategic push into high-intensity defense markets and may strengthen its standing with security organizations seeking reliable, low-latency video capabilities in bandwidth-constrained, complex environments.

data.description:

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

An update from ETRACS Alerian MLP Index ETN Class B ( (AMUB) ) is now available.

On March 31, 2026, UBS Group AG and UBS AG reported the final outcomes of two rescission offers covering specific past debt securities transactions that ran from February 19 to March 20, 2026. These offers allowed certain investors to unwind previous purchases of UBS Group AG and UBS Americas Inc. notes under defined conditions.

For the UBS Group AG rescission offer, investors submitted valid claims on about $2.801 million in principal amount of securities, with settlement scheduled around April 2, 2026, indicating only a modest portion of eligible notes was returned. In contrast, no valid claims were submitted under the UBS Americas rescission offer, suggesting limited investor demand to unwind those positions and implying minimal impact on UBS’s overall funding profile and outstanding debt structure.

More about ETRACS Alerian MLP Index ETN Class B

UBS Group AG and UBS AG are global financial institutions headquartered in Zurich and Basel, Switzerland, operating across investment banking, wealth management, and asset management. data.description:
End of Quarter Sale - 50% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

ETRACS Alerian MLP Index ETN Class B ( (AMUB) ) has provided an update.

On March 31, 2026, UBS Group AG and UBS AG reported the final results of two rescission offers launched on February 19, 2026 and closed on March 20, 2026, aimed at certain purchasers of specified UBS Group and UBS Americas Inc. debt securities. The announcement details how many investors chose to unwind prior purchases and receive their money back, clarifying the scale of the liability for the bank.

For the UBS Group rescission offer, valid claims were submitted and not withdrawn for approximately $2.801 million in principal amount of securities, with settlement expected around April 2, 2026. UBS also disclosed that no valid, unwithdrawn claims were submitted under the separate UBS Americas rescission offer, signaling limited take-up and a modest direct financial impact from these corrective actions for the group and its noteholders.

More about ETRACS Alerian MLP Index ETN Class B

UBS Group AG and UBS AG are global financial services institutions headquartered in Zurich and Basel, Switzerland. They operate across investment banking, wealth management, and asset management, issuing and guaranteeing a range of debt securities for institutional and retail investors in international capital markets.

data.description:
End of Quarter Sale - 50% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

Zhibao Technology Inc. Class A ( (ZBAO) ) has provided an announcement.

Zhibao Technology Inc., a Cayman Islands–registered InsurTech holding company, runs its main operations in China through subsidiaries Zhibao China and Sunshine Insurance Brokers, where it has pioneered a 2B2C digital embedded insurance model. By integrating customized insurance solutions into the online ecosystems of over 3,100 business and institutional partners, the company serves more than 27 million end customers with scenario‑specific brokerage and MGU services powered by its proprietary PaaS, big data and AI.

In a Form 6‑K filed on March 31, 2026, Zhibao reported unaudited results for the six months ended December 31, 2025, showing revenue rising 41% year‑on‑year to RMB 206.0 million and a swing from a small net loss to a modest net profit of RMB 0.6 million, underscoring improving operating performance. Over the same period, the company continued to fund growth via a convertible‑note arrangement with investor L1, settling part of the debt through share issuance and cash while leaving a small balance outstanding, and on September 30, 2025, acquired 51% of Zhonglian for RMB 25.5 million, a move that expands its footprint ahead of further 2B2C channel and technology‑driven expansion in China’s early‑stage embedded insurance market.

The most recent analyst rating on (ZBAO) stock is a Hold with a $0.76 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on Zhibao Technology Inc. Class A stock, see the ZBAO Stock Forecast page.

More about Zhibao Technology Inc. Class A

Zhibao Technology Inc. is a Cayman Islands–incorporated holding company that operates a leading high‑growth InsurTech business in China through its PRC subsidiaries, including Zhibao China and Sunshine Insurance Brokers. It focuses on 2B2C digital embedded insurance, delivering customized insurance brokerage and managing general underwriter services via proprietary PaaS technology to over 3,100 business channels and more than 27 million end customers across sectors such as travel, sports, logistics, utilities and e‑commerce.

Under its model, Zhibao represents end customers as their authorized broker, partnering with more than 100 insurance companies to embed tailored products into the digital touchpoints of corporate and government clients. The company leverages big data and AI to iterate over 40 scenario‑specific digital insurance solutions, positioning itself as an early mover in China’s nascent embedded insurance brokerage market and aiming to expand both its business channel base and direct‑to‑consumer capabilities.

Global-E Online clarified on March 31, 2026 that its recently filed 2025 annual report contained a scrivener’s error regarding the contribution of existing merchants to GMV. data.description:
Farmhouse ( (FMHS) ) has released a notification of late filing.

End of Quarter Sale - 50% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions

Farmhouse, Inc. has filed a Form 12b-25 to notify investors that it will delay its Form 10-K (Yearly Report) for the fiscal year ended December 31, 2025. The company is based in San Francisco and the late filing relates specifically to its annual report.

Management cites the need for additional time to complete preparation and review of its financial statements, including related disclosures and audit procedures. The delay is framed as an effort to avoid unreasonable effort or expense in meeting the original deadline.

Farmhouse, Inc. states that it expects to file the Form 10-K (Yearly Report) on or before April 15, 2026, which falls within the 15-day extension window allowed under SEC Rule 12b-25. Investors should therefore anticipate the annual results becoming publicly available within that grace period.

The company does not anticipate any significant change in results of operations compared with the prior fiscal year, according to the notification. No preliminary financial figures are provided, and the statements remain forward-looking until the full audited Form 10-K (Yearly Report) is filed.

Farmhouse, Inc. confirms that all other required periodic reports over the past 12 months have been filed on time, signaling ongoing compliance with SEC reporting rules. The notification is signed by Chief Executive Officer Evan Horowitz on March 31, 2026, underscoring executive responsibility for the delayed filing and the plan to complete it within the allowed extension.

data.description:

The latest announcement is out from Professional Diversity ( (IPDN) ).

Professional Diversity Network reported on March 31, 2026 that its fiscal 2025 revenue slipped 2.7% to about $6.55 million, as softer hiring demand and lower employer spending weighed on its TalentAlly and NAPW networks, while RemoteMore’s contracted software development revenue rose sharply. Despite cutting sales and marketing costs, the company’s net loss from continuing operations widened to roughly $6.51 million on higher costs and surging general and administrative expenses, as it raised more capital and committed nearly $9.5 million to copyright-related and digital token assets to reposition itself toward technology- and blockchain-enabled growth.

In the fourth quarter of 2025, TalentAlly and NAPW revenues fell double digits year on year, while RemoteMore posted more than 50% growth, but higher costs drove the quarterly net loss from continuing operations up 272% to about $2.9 million. The balance sheet showed total assets more than doubling to about $17.9 million, driven largely by long-term investments, against a sharp drop in cash, higher current liabilities and an increase in stockholders’ equity, underscoring a strategic but costly pivot that could pressure near-term results while aiming to enhance long-term positioning.

data.title: INNOVATE Corp Gains EU CE Mark for MediBeacon Devices
data.description:
End of Quarter Sale - 50% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

An announcement from INNOVATE Corp ( (VATE) ) is now available.

On March 31, 2026, INNOVATE Corp. reported that its equity-method investee MediBeacon received European Union CE Mark certification under the EU Medical Device Regulation for its TGFR Monitor and TGFR Reusable Sensor, two Class IIa devices used in a transdermal system to assess kidney function. This certification, which complements existing approvals for the broader TGFR System including the Lumitrace injection in the U.S. and China, is expected to enable use of the technology in European clinical research and strengthens MediBeacon’s regulatory and technological position in renal diagnostics within INNOVATE’s life sciences portfolio.

The CE Mark confirms that the MediBeacon TGFR hardware meets stringent EU safety, quality and performance standards, allowing integration with the already CE-marked TGFR Disposable Ring as part of a point-of-care system that measures glomerular filtration rate via fluorescent tracer clearance. With clinicians in Europe preparing to incorporate transdermal GFR into research later in 2026, the move signals growing clinical validation and potential adoption of MediBeacon’s kidney function monitoring platform, which could enhance the value of INNOVATE’s life sciences segment while highlighting execution and IP-protection risks noted by the company.

The most recent analyst rating on (VATE) stock is a Hold with a $4.50 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on INNOVATE Corp stock, see the VATE Stock Forecast page.

Spark’s Take on VATE Stock

According to Spark, TipRanks’ AI Analyst, VATE is a Neutral.

The score is held back mainly by high leverage with minimal equity and continued losses despite improved 2025 cash generation. Earnings commentary and recent results point to strong top-line momentum and backlog/approval-driven opportunities, but margin pressure and debt levels keep risk elevated. Technicals are broadly neutral and valuation is weak due to negative earnings and no dividend support.

To see Spark’s full report on VATE stock, click here.

More about INNOVATE Corp

INNOVATE Corp., listed on the NYSE under the ticker VATE, is a diversified holding company with a portfolio of assets in three key segments of the new economy: Infrastructure, Life Sciences and Spectrum. The company, which employs roughly 3,700 people across its subsidiaries, focuses on stakeholder capitalism and holds an equity stake in MediBeacon Inc., a medical technology firm specializing in fluorescent tracer agents and transdermal detection for organ function measurement.

MediBeacon, based in St. Louis with additional operations in Mannheim, Germany, develops proprietary fluorescent tracer agents and sensor technologies designed to provide clinically actionable measurements of organ function, especially kidney function. Its MediBeacon TGFR System, which includes the Lumitrace injection, sensors and algorithms, is approved for human use by regulators in the U.S. and China, and the company holds an extensive global patent portfolio covering these technologies and exploratory applications in fields like gastroenterology, ophthalmology and surgery.

CoreWeave ( (CRWV) ) has issued an update.

On March 30, 2026, CoreWeave’s subsidiary CoreWeave Compute Acquisition Co. VIII, LLC entered into an $8.5 billion delayed draw term loan facility arranged by MUFG, Morgan Stanley, and others, primarily to fund GPU servers and infrastructure needed to perform a major AI cloud customer contract. The facility, which matures in March 2032 and is secured by substantially all assets of the borrowing vehicle and guaranteed on a limited basis by CoreWeave, allows draws through June 2027 under both floating-rate and fixed-rate tranches and is governed by standard covenants, including a minimum 1.15x debt service coverage ratio.

In a March 31, 2026 announcement, CoreWeave said the DDTL 4.0 Facility received A3 and A (low) investment-grade ratings from Moody’s and DBRS, the first such rating for a GPU- and HPC infrastructure-backed loan tied to an AI customer contract, and was meaningfully oversubscribed by global institutional investors. The structure initially provides about $7.5 billion of borrowing capacity, expandable to $8.5 billion as assets stabilize, reducing the company’s cost of capital, fulfilling financing needs for previously contracted AI cloud services, and further strengthening its credit profile and expansion momentum in the AI infrastructure market.

Prologis ( (PLD) ) just unveiled an announcement.

On March 26, 2026, Prologis, L.P. and affiliated borrowers entered into an Amended and Restated Global Senior Credit Agreement providing a revolving, multi-currency credit facility of approximately $3 billion, with an accordion feature of up to an additional $1 billion, split between a $2 billion U.S. Dollar Tranche and a €864.2 million Euro Tranche. The facility, maturing June 28, 2030 with options to extend to as late as June 30, 2031, is priced at a spread that was 65 basis points at closing, includes customary financial covenants and cross-acceleration defaults, and is fully guaranteed by the Operating Partnership, while leaving Prologis, Inc. unrequired to guarantee unless it takes on new indebtedness, and a concurrent amendment aligned key terms of a separate 2025 global credit facility with the new 2026 structure.

These changes bolster Prologis’ liquidity, extend its debt maturity profile, and harmonize documentation across major bank facilities, likely enhancing financial flexibility and reducing refinancing risk for stakeholders.

IShares Bitcoin Trust Registered ( (IBIT) ) has provided an update.

On March 27, 2026, Jay Jacobs, a Managing Director at BlackRock and U.S. Head of Equity ETFs since April 7, 2025, was appointed to the board of iShares Delaware Trust Sponsor LLC and named its President and Chief Executive Officer, placing a seasoned ETF strategist at the helm of the sponsor of the iShares Bitcoin Trust ETF. Jacobs brings extensive experience from BlackRock, Global X ETFs, and the New York Stock Exchange, while on the same date former President and CEO Shannon Ghia resigned from the sponsor and its board, with the company stating her departure did not stem from any disagreement over the trust’s operations or policies.

The leadership change underscores BlackRock’s intention to align the bitcoin trust sponsor more closely with its core ETF expertise and strategic direction, potentially reinforcing product development and client engagement in the fast-growing crypto-related ETF segment. Stakeholders in the iShares Bitcoin Trust ETF may view the appointment as strengthening governance and strategic oversight, given Jacobs’ background in equity and thematic ETFs and the confirmation of an orderly, non-contentious transition from Ghia’s tenure.

More about IShares Bitcoin Trust Registered

iShares Bitcoin Trust ETF is a digital asset investment vehicle sponsored by iShares Delaware Trust Sponsor LLC, an affiliate within the BlackRock group that focuses on exchange-traded fund products. The trust provides investors with exposure to bitcoin through a regulated ETF structure, aligning with BlackRock’s broader strategy in index and thematic exchange-traded funds.

data.title: Vale to Distribute R$700 Million in 2H25 Participating Debenture Premiums on March 31, 2026
data.description:
End of Quarter Sale - 50% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

Vale SA ( (VALE) ) just unveiled an announcement.

Vale S.A. reported that it would pay R$700.46 million in premiums on participating debentures on March 31, 2026, related to performance in the second half of 2025, with financial settlement scheduled for April 1, 2026. The payment, equal to R$2.341507529 per debenture and up sharply from R$598.34 million in the first half of 2025, is linked mainly to 1.8% of net revenue from iron ore sales and 1.25% from copper concentrate at the Sossego mine, reflecting higher iron ore volumes and revenues in both its Northern and Southeastern Systems during 2H25.

The company detailed that 2H25 iron ore sales reached 148.6 Mt, driving R$875.5 million in iron ore-linked premiums, while copper-related premiums added R$34.28 million, and annual per-debenture premiums have trended higher over the past decade. The announcement underscores how Vale’s strong iron ore revenue generation continues to translate into sizeable recurring cash flows for holders of its legacy participating debentures, although these payments remain subject to Brazilian income tax treatment according to each investor’s status.

The most recent analyst rating on (VALE) stock is a Hold with a $16.00 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on Vale SA stock, see the VALE Stock Forecast page.

Spark’s Take on VALE Stock

According to Spark, TipRanks’ AI Analyst, VALE is a Neutral.

The score is primarily supported by stable financial footing and a constructive technical setup (price above key moving averages with positive momentum), plus a favorable earnings-call tone with reiterated guidance and disciplined capital allocation. Offsetting these are materially weaker post-peak profitability and cash flows, and a less attractive valuation for a cyclical company due to the high P/E despite the strong dividend yield.

To see Spark’s full report on VALE stock, click here.

More about Vale SA

Vale S.A. is a Brazilian diversified mining and metals company, with core operations in iron ore and base metals such as copper. It is a major global supplier of iron ore products from systems in northern and southeastern Brazil, and also produces copper concentrates from assets including the Sossego mine, making it a key player in global commodity and steel supply chains.
